title: acer negundo var. normalis
intro: Acer negundo var. normalis, also known as the box elder or Manitoba maple, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its compound leaves and smooth, grey bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Acer negundo var. normalis include reducing fever, alleviating arthritis symptoms, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
